title: Hot and Sour Soup

seo.title: Authentic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe | Easy Chinese Soup
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Chinese Hot and Sour Soup with our easy-to-follow recipe. Get authentic flavors and a perfect balance of spicy, sour, and savory.

easy Hot and Sour Soup recipe

seo.h1: Authentic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe
h2Headings: What is Hot and Sour Soup?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Chinese Hot and Sour Soup with mushrooms, tofu, and bamboo shoots, garnished with green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a delicious bowl of homemade Hot and Sour Soup, bursting with traditional Chinese flavors.

description: Experience the exhilarating flavors of authentic Hot and Sour Soup, a beloved Chinese classic that perfectly balances fiery spice with a tangy kick. This comforting and robust soup is packed with savory ingredients like tender pork, silky tofu, and earthy mushrooms, all swimming in a rich, aromatic broth. It is an invigorating culinary journey that promises warmth and satisfaction with every spoonful.

ingredients.notes: mixed with 4 tablespoons cold water for slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rehydrate dried shiitake and wood ear mushrooms in warm water for 20-30 minutes. Drain, squeeze out excess water, remove stems from shiitake, and slice both mushrooms thinly. Slice pork loin and bamboo shoots. Dice tofu. Prepare cornstarch slurry by mixing cornstarch with cold water. Beat eggs.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat a small amount of oil over medium heat. Add grated ginger and minced garlic, sautéing for about 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer Broth and Solids
instructions.text: Pour in chicken or vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then add the sliced pork, shiitake mushrooms, wood ear mushrooms, and bamboo shoots. Reduce heat to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the pork is cooked through.

instructions.title: Flavor the Soup
instructions.text: Stir in the rice vinegar, soy sauce, and white pepper. Add the diced firm tofu. Taste and adjust the seasonings, adding more vinegar or white pepper for desired sourness and heat.

instructions.title: Thicken and Add Egg
instructions.text: Slowly pour the cornstarch slurry into the simmering soup while stirring continuously until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. Once thickened, slowly drizzle the beaten eggs into the soup in a thin stream, stirring gently with a fork or chopsticks to create delicate egg ribbons.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the soup from heat. Stir in the sesame oil. Ladle the hot and sour soup into individual bowls. Garnish generously with chopped green onions before serving immediately.

tips: For extra depth of flavor, use a good quality homemade chicken broth. Adjust the amount of white pepper and vinegar gradually to achieve your perfect balance of heat and sourness.
To make this soup vegetarian, substitute pork with extra mushrooms or rehydrated dried lily flowers, and use vegetable broth. Ensure your soy sauce is mushroom-based or a vegetarian variety.

Hot and Sour Soup is best enjoyed fresh. If reheating, gently warm it on the stovetop and add a splash of broth or water if it has thickened too much. Garnish with fresh green onions just before serving.
article: Hot and Sour Soup is a beloved Chinese Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
